Name: adaptive_block_size
Title: Adaptive Block Size: Dynamic Context Window Adjustment for Efficient Training
Experiment: Modify the model to dynamically adjust its block size during training, starting with a smaller block size and gradually increasing it. This could potentially lead to faster initial training and better long-range dependency learning.

Name: layerwise_learning_rates
Title: Layer-wise Learning Rate Adaptation: Optimizing Training Dynamics in Transformer Models
Experiment: Implement layer-wise learning rates, where each transformer layer has its own learning rate. Modify the configure_optimizers function to assign different learning rates to different layers, with deeper layers having lower learning rates. Compare the training dynamics, convergence speed, and final performance with the baseline model.

Name: layerwise_dropout
Title: Layer-wise Dropout Adjustment: Enhancing Regularization in Transformer Models
Experiment: Modify the Block class to accept individual dropout rate parameters for each layer. Implement an incremental evaluation framework (e.g., grid search) to systematically identify and adjust optimal dropout rates during training, based on epoch or validation loss. Compare the performance of models with fixed dropout rates against those with dynamically optimized rates. Track validation loss and accuracy to assess the impact of layer-wise dropout adjustments on model generalization.

Name: dynamic_token_sampling
Title: Dynamic Token Sampling: Adaptive Inference Strategies for Enhanced Text Generation
Experiment: Modify the generate function in the GPT class to evaluate the generated context after a set of tokens using a simple coherence evaluation mechanism. Implement a fallback strategy where, if context quality cannot be determined, the model defaults to a conservative sampling method. Compare the quality of generated text against traditional static sampling methods across various prompts and datasets.

Name: frequency_based_token_weighting
Title: Frequency-Based Token Weighting: Enhancing Token Representations in Language Models
Experiment: Modify the tokenization process to include a frequency-based weighting mechanism for tokens. Implement a function that adjusts the weights of tokens based on their frequency in the training data at regular intervals. Compare the performance of the model with frequency-weighted tokens against a baseline using traditional static encodings, evaluating the coherence and fluency of generated text across various prompts.

Name: attention_visualization
Title: Attention Visualization: Enhancing Interpretability in Language Models
Experiment: Modify the `generate` function in the GPT class to extract attention weights for the last generated token only. Create a new function to visualize these weights, mapping them back to the input context. This will allow users to see how the model's attention shifts for the most recent prediction, providing insights into model reasoning. Evaluate results based on user feedback on interpretability and coherence of the generated text.
